title: 4 Managing a User Management Security Officer
body_text:
4	Managing a User Management Security Officer
4.1	Overview of Managing a User Management Security Officer
User Management Security Officers (Security Officers) create, modify, and delete Client Centre Users in the User Management application. Security Officers can also delete and modify other Security Officers in the User Management application, including assigning administrative rights to applications (which gives the Security Officer the ability to assign those applications to others).
Authorized representatives of your financial institution appoint Security Officers. Since User Management primarily operates in a dual custody environment, financial institutions require a minimum of two Security Officers. When determining the number of Security Officers that you require, ensure that you consider coverage for absences.
Note:	Some applications don芒聙聶t require dual authorization in User Management. See Chapter 8, Managing Application Access and Permissions, for specific application requirements.
4.2	Adding a User Management Security Officer
Security Officers can芒聙聶t create other Security Officers. These requests must be sent to Central 1.
Add a Security Officer as follows:
Complete a CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918, for each new Security Officer. See Figure 1.

Example:	If a Security Officer has administrative rights to the Wires application, they can assign other Client Centre Users and other Security Officers access to the application and/or assign administrative rights to the application to another Security Officer. The Security Officer won芒聙聶t have access to the Wires application directly unless Wires is also listed under the Personal Access Rights section of the form.

Figure 1: CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918
Forward the form as directed and retain a copy for your records.
Central聽1 will communicate a unique one-time password to the new Security Officer. See Chapter 11, Password Registration and Client Centre Login Process, for procedures on registering your Client Centre password.
Have another Security Officer assign a 2-step security token to the new Security Officer. See Chapter 7, Managing 2-Step Security Tokens, for procedures.
Related Information
CPS Admin Appointment Certificate,
Form 1918
4.3	Modifying or Deleting a User Management Security Officer
There are two ways to manage your Security Officers. You can either send a written request to Central 1 to make a change on your behalf, or you can make the change directly in the User Management application. For the most part, Security Officers can modify and delete other Security Officers in User Management. This includes the following functions:
Adding or deleting personal access to applications. See Chapter 8, Managing Application Access and Permissions, for procedures.
Assigning or deleting administrative rights to applications (i.e., giving a Security Officer the ability to assign applications to other Client Centre Users and Security Officers, or removing that ability from the individual). To do so, the Security Officer must also have administrative rights to the application(s).
Note:	See Section 4.4, Assigning Administrative Rights to a User Management Security Officer, for procedures on assigning administrative rights to other Security Officers.
Adding, removing, and unlocking a 2-step security token. See Chapter 7, Managing 2-Step Security Tokens.
Updating the status of a Security Officer (e.g., from 芒聙聹Locked芒聙聺 to 芒聙聹Active芒聙聺). See Section 6.5, Changing a User芒聙聶s Account Status, for procedures on updating the status via User Management.
Important
:	Ensure that a locked status isn芒聙聶t the result of an unauthorized login attempt.
Updating the Security Officer芒聙聶s name or other personal information. The user ID isn芒聙聶t editable. See Section 6.4, Updating a User芒聙聶s Contact Information, for procedures.
Deleting a Security Officer. See Section 6.7, Deleting a User, for procedures.
The only change Security Officers can芒聙聶t make to other Security Officers via the User Management application is resetting their password (only available via Central 1).
Note:	Security Officers can reset their own password in User Management. Another Officer must authorize the change. See Section  6.6, Resetting a User芒聙聶s Password, for procedures.
Modify or delete a Security Officer via Central 1 as follows:
Complete the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035. See Figure 2.

Delete Security Officer
. Deleting a Client Centre user ID is permanent. The deleted user ID can芒聙聶t be used again.

4.4	Assigning Administrative Rights to a User Management Security Officer
Administrative rights allows a Security Officer to assign Client Centre Users and other Security Officers access to an application. A Security Officer can only assign administrative rights to another Security Officer if they also have administrative rights to the application.
Assign administrative rights to a Security Officer as follows:
Open the user芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Add
in the Admin column next to the appropriate application. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Add Administration Rights to an Application
Select
OK
when prompted.
Adding administrative access to most applications requires approval from another Security Officer. See the appropriate section in this chapter for application-specific requirements. See Chapter 9, Managing Requests, for procedures on approving requests.
4.5	Reviewing User Management Security Officers for Inactivity and Appropriate Access
To reduce the risk of unauthorized access to User Management, and the applications it protects, it芒聙聶s important to regularly review all Security Officers.
Your financial institution is responsible for ensuring that its Security Officers are:
Deleted if they no longer require User Management access, or if they芒聙聶re no longer employees of your financial institution.
Note: 	 Security Officers should be deleted immediately upon termination of employment.
Assigned to applications that accurately reflect their job responsibilities.
To conduct reviews of Security Officers, view and, if necessary, update the individual in User Management as described in Chapter 6, Managing and Deleting Users. Alternatively, evaluate the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918, and CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Requests, Form 2035, that you submitted. Ensure that the information recorded on the forms is still valid. Submit a CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request as directed on the form to change the applications to which a Security Officer has access or to delete a Security Officer.
Central 1 reviews the last login date of each Security Officer regularly. Central 1 disables Security Officers who haven芒聙聶t logged in to User Management for 13 months. After a further 90 days Central聽1 deletes the user芒聙聶s account. A deleted account can be recovered for up to 90 days.
Related Information
CPS Admin Appointment Certificate,
Form 1918
CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request,
Form 2035
END
